items.title: VI Live: AZ stevent af op finaleplek, Man City en Arsenal op voorsprong
items.summary: AZ en Telstar nemen het woensdagavond om 20.00 uur tegen elkaar op in de halve finale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker. Daarnaast is er volop voetbal in het buitenland, onder meer in de Premier League, Bundesliga, Copa del Rey en Coppa Italia. Mis niets in deze VI Live.

items.title: Newcastle United v Manchester United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 8.15pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Table | Follow us on Bluesky | And mail Scott ⚽ Brighton v Arsenal, Man City v Nottingham Forest – live 2 min: Newcastle get onto the front foot immediately. Hall bombs down the left; Trippier probes down the right. Shaw looks to have tugged Trippier back, but the referee waves play on. Not too much in the way of fume from the players, but the fans aren’t happy that’s for sure. Manchester United get the ball rolling. They’re kicking towards the Gallowgate in this first half. Continue reading...

items.title: Brighton v Arsenal, Manchester City v Nottingham Forest, and more: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Latest news from four games kicking off at 7.30pm GMT ⚽ Live scores | Table | Follow us on Bluesky | And mail Niall ⚽ Newcastle v Manchester United – follow it live Some pre-game chat from Pep Guardiola, who confirms Nico O’Reilly is out of the squad after “feeling uncomfortable” in training with a knock. “We can only focus on ourselves for the rest of the games … I don’t know what is going to happen. Today is how we behave against Forest. Haaland has made an incredible impact since he arrived years ago. In the first quarter of this season he was outstanding. We need everyone. Everyone has to be ready.” Continue reading...

items.title: Maguire given suspended sentence by Greek court
items.summary: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire will appeal to Greece's Supreme Court after a 15-month suspended sentence was imposed.

items.title: Man United's Fletcher apologises for anti-gay slur
items.summary: Manchester United youngster Jack Fletcher has been charged by the Football Association (FA) for using an anti-gay slur.

items.title: Sources: Atlético's Griezmann pauses MLS plans
items.summary: Atlético Madrid forward Antoine Griezmann has put a potential move to MLS club Orlando City on hold this season now that the Spanish club has advanced to the Copa del Rey final on April 18, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Leroux teases Angel City return after season off
items.summary: Angel City FC forward Sydney Leroux teased a return to the field in a social media post on Tuesday after missing all of last year with an excused absence.

items.title: Uitstekende cijfers: Timber meest productieve verdediger in de Premier League
items.summary: Jurriën Timber is dit seizoen een opvallend productieve verdediger in de Premier League. De 24-jarige verdediger van Arsenal was woensdag in de wedstrijd tegen Brighton & Hove Albion opnieuw belangrijk met een assist en was dit seizoen acht keer direct betrokken bij een doelpunt. Er is geen verdediger in de Premier League die dat aantal dit seizoen kan evenaren.

items.title: Perez wil niet spreken van succesjaar voor PSV: 'Alleen landstitel is te weinig'
items.summary: Kenneth Perez vindt niet dat PSV tevreden mag zijn met dit seizoen. De Eindhovenaren lijken eenvoudig kampioen te worden, maar werden dinsdag in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker uitgeschakeld door NEC.

items.title: Man United-middenvelder Fletcher voor zes duels geschorst wegens homofobe taal
items.summary: Manchester United-middenvelder Jack Fletcher (18) is voor zes wedstrijden geschorst na een homofobe belediging tijdens een wedstrijd. De achttienjarige Engelsman heeft de straf geaccepteerd.

items.title: Juventus hengelt naar Roma-uitblinker Malen, Van de Ven gelinkt aan Barcelona
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Premier League live updates: Saka gives Arsenal lead vs. Brighton, City lead Forest
items.summary: Enjoy all the action from Wednesday's Premier League matches, including Brighton & Hove Albion vs. Arsenal and Manchester City vs. Nottingham Forest.

items.title: Intercontinental World Cup playoffs in doubt as Iraq squad face travel chaos
items.summary: Iraq team due in Mexico for playoff final on 31 March Middle East crisis has made travel plans uncertain The intercontinental World Cup playoffs are in doubt with officials from the Iraq Football Association (IFA) in crisis talks with Fifa over concerns they may be unable to take part in the final scheduled for Mexico later this month. The Guardian has learned that the IFA received a letter from Iraq’s national airline, Iraqi Airways, and the Ministry of Transportation informing them that the country’s airspace will remain closed for “at least four weeks”, which would leave around 40% of the squad unable to travel. Continue reading...

items.title: 'A miracle to walk' - Pavan discharged from hospital after lift shaft fall
items.summary: Italian golfer Andrea Pavan has been discharged from hospital days after falling down a lift shaft in South Africa.

items.title: Can Jacks finish India - and will Buttler finally make a start?
items.summary: Finishing batting innings has not been a problem for England at this T20 World Cup and that is largely down to Will Jacks.

items.title: Nisbet penalty levels the score for Aberdeen LIVE! & highlights
Arsenal in front but Brighton pushing for leveller LIVE!
'Special' Allen century exposes familiar South African failings
items.summary: New Zealand skipper Mitchell Santner hailed Finn Allen's "special", match-winning century in their T20 World Cup semi-final against South Africa, while the Proteas captain cursed another "bad night" in a knockout match at a major tournament.

items.title: Harry Maguire given 15-month suspended prison sentence after retrial in Greece
items.summary: Player was first found guilty after incident in 2020 England defender to appeal again against verdict A Greek appeals court has found the England and Manchester United defender Harry Maguire guilty of aggravated assault, upholding an earlier conviction that effectively ends a six-year-long legal battle to clear his name. A three-member panel of judges sitting on the Aegean island of Syros handed Maguire a 15-month and 20-day suspended prison term citing his role in a bar brawl on Mykonos in 2020. Originally he had received a 21-month and 10-day suspended sentence. The higher court also added a non-negotiable €1,500 fine. Continue reading...

items.title: Maguire given reduced 15-month suspended sentence for Greece incident
items.summary: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire has his suspended sentence reduced to 15 months at a retrial following an incident in Greece in 2020.

items.title: Galatasaray fans banned from Liverpool second leg
items.summary: Galatasaray fans are banned from the club's Champions League visit to Liverpool after disturbances during their game with Juventus in the previous round.

items.title: WSL’s promotion-relegation playoff will give WSL 2 side home advantage
items.summary: Fixture will be a first in English women’s top divisions Dates and kick-off times revealed for WSL season finale The Women’s Super League 2 side involved in the new promotion-relegation playoff will have home advantage in the one-leg contest, WSL Football has confirmed, with the top-flight side having to play away from home. The first fixture of its kind in the English women’s top divisions, the playoff will be held on Saturday 23 May with a 12.30pm kick-off and will pit the team third in WSL 2 against the WSL’s bottom side. The match will be broadcast live on BBC Two and Sky Sports. Continue reading...

items.title: Liverpool top-five failure 'could cost £120m'
items.summary: Failure to qualify for the Champions League would have financial implications for Liverpool. How much would it impact them?

items.title: Fletcher banned for six games for homophobic slur
items.summary: Manchester United midfielder Jack Fletcher apologises for using a homophobic slur that has led to him receiving a six-match ban.

items.title: 'It's better to be chased' - the mentality of a title race
items.summary: The Premier League title race is set to go to the wire. BBC Sport has spoken to former winners to find out how players deal with the pressure.

items.title: Condamné par la justice grecque : le coéquipier de Senne Lammens ne lâche pas l'affaire
items.summary: L'affaire de Mykonos n'est pas terminée pour Harry Maguire. La justice grecque a rendu une nouvelle décision, mais le défenseur de Manchester United compte continuer le combat.

items.title: Wout Faes heeft knoop doorgehakt over zijn toekomst bij AS Monaco
items.summary: Wout Faes kwam deze winter naar Monaco om de vele blessures op te vangen en stapelt er speelminuten op. De Rode Duivel is duidelijk over zijn toekomst.

items.title: Elleboogtik heeft grote gevolgen: dit is de definitieve straf voor Westerlo-speler Nsiala
items.summary: Clinton Nsiala kreeg afgelopen weekend rood na een elleboogstoot tegen Union SG. Westerlo hoopte de straf nog te milderen, maar het Disciplinair Comité ging daar niet in mee. De verdediger kent nu zijn definitieve sanctie.

items.title: Pas de pitié pour Van Den Kerkhof : le Comité disciplinaire a lourdement sanctionné le joueur de Charleroi
items.summary: Charleroi a tenté de contester, mais le Comité Disciplinaire a rendu son verdict. Kevin Van Den Kerkhof ne disputera plus la phase classique du championnat.

items.title: Geweldig nieuws voor Standard uit de ziekenboeg
items.summary: Matthieu Epolo is terug op training en zal binnenkort opnieuw zijn plaats onder de lat bij Standard innemen. Goed nieuws voor Vincent Euvrard, want Lucas Pirard stelde tijdens zijn afwezigheid niet altijd gerust.
